input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward request that doesn't require exploring a codebase.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="My MAUI App">

    <!-- ==================== Dashboard (single page) ==================== -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- ============ Products (two tabs: Active / Archived) ============= -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                Icon="icon_active.png"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                Icon="icon_archived.png"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- ========================== Settings ============================= -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**AppShell.xaml** — Three `FlyoutItem`s:

| Flyout entry | What it shows |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| **Products** | A `Tab` with two `ShellContent` children → bottom tabs for **Active** / **Archived** |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a route so you can navigate to it from either product list with:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

Key points:
- Wrapping two `ShellContent`s inside a `<Tab>` creates **bottom tabs** within the Products flyout item.
- `ProductDetailsPage` is registered via `Routing.RegisterRoute` because it's a **detail page pushed onto the navigation stack**, not a top-level Shell destination.
- Pass data using query parameters and `[QueryProperty]` or `IQueryAttributable` on the target page.
